task.name: CEO Approval Gate
task.objective: Enforce formal CEO approval for major efforts, ideas, and strategic decisions before any work begins

llm.mandate: NEVER skip this gate for qualifying work — the CEO's time is valuable but their approval is non-negotiable for major efforts
Proposals must be concise, actionable, and include cost/risk assessment
The CEO can approve, reject, modify, or defer — respect all four outcomes

requires-approval.criterion: New feature or epic that doesn't exist in an approved plan
Architecture change that affects more than 2 system components
Any work estimated at more than 1 sprint of effort
Security posture changes (new auth flows, data exposure changes, permission model changes)
Technology adoption (new frameworks, services, or infrastructure)
Scope changes to approved epics or stories
Data model changes that affect multiple domains
Any creative or branding direction decisions
Agent team restructuring or new agent creation
Budget implications (paid services, compute costs, licensing)

autonomous-work.desc: Agents can proceed without CEO approval
autonomous-work.criterion: Bug fixes within existing approved stories
Implementation of tasks within approved stories
Code review and testing of approved work
Refactoring that doesn't change behavior or architecture
Security patches for known vulnerabilities in approved code

proposal-format.mandate: All proposals MUST follow this exact format when presented to the CEO
proposal-format.template: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ⚔️ PROPOSAL FOR CEO APPROVAL │ │ Submitted by: {agent_name} via Maestro │ │ Date: {date} │ │ Priority: {CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW} │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ WHAT: [1-2 sentence description of the proposal] │ │ │ │ WHY: [Business justification — what problem does │ │ this solve or what opportunity does it seize?] │ │ │ │ HOW: [High-level approach — not implementation │ │ details, but strategy] │ │ │ │ SCOPE: [What's in, what's out] │ │ │ │ AGENTS NEEDED: [Which team members and roles] │ │ │ │ EFFORT: [Estimated sprints / complexity] │ │ │ │ RISKS: [Top 2-3 risks with mitigation strategies] │ │ │ │ SECURITY IMPACT: [How this affects security posture] │ │ │ │ DEPENDENCIES: [What must exist or happen first] │ │ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ CEO ACTION REQUIRED: │ │ [A] Approve — proceed with this plan │ │ [R] Reject — explain concerns, agent will revise │ │ [M] Modify — specify changes, agent will adjust │ │ [D] Defer — table for later, note when to revisit │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

step.title: Classify the Work
step.action: Evaluate the proposed work against the classification criteria above

check.action: Proceed without CEO approval — note in agent memory that work is within approved scope
HALT all implementation work — prepare a formal proposal

step.title: Prepare Proposal
step.action: Gather all relevant context about the proposed work
Format the proposal using the template above

step.title: Present to CEO
step.action: Display the formatted proposal to the CEO
WAIT for CEO response — do NOT proceed until one of [A], [R], [M], [D] is received

check.action: Record approval in proposal file with timestamp
Update agent memory with approved scope
Broadcast to relevant agents: "CEO approved: {proposal title}"
Ask CEO if they want a revised proposal or to drop the idea entirely
Record deferral with revisit date (ask CEO when to revisit)
Maestro will resurface deferred proposals on their revisit date

red-flags.flag: Agent attempting to start major work without checking this gate
Scope creep — work expanding beyond what was originally approved
Agent reframing major work as "minor" to avoid approval
Multiple small proposals that collectively represent one large unapproved effort
